pi_items.verified_value: 4StoreyBuilding.inp exists in /examples/equest_env/data/ directory; path indicates Windows environment-specific location

South-facing walls in standard compass convention should have azimuth 180°. However, current model has AZIMUTH=360 (North) at building/floor level; south walls do not explicitly override this. Metadata value is correct for 'south-facing' definition but model may not be properly configured.
pi_items.source: Grep search in 4StoreyBuilding.inp: Found AZIMUTH=360 at FLOOR and BUILD-PARAMETERS levels, no explicit AZIMUTH=180 on south walls. Standard building orientation convention: 0/360=North, 90=East, 180=South, 270=West

privileged_info_summary: The south_window_overhangs task operates on a synthetic 4StoreyBuilding model containing 5 south-facing windows (including 1 door) distributed across 3 occupied floors: 2 windows+1 door on Ground floor, 1 window on Middle floor, 1 window on Top floor. The task requires adding horizontal overhangs with specifications: depth 3.0 ft (tolerance ±0.2 ft) and height above window 0.5 ft (tolerance ±0.1 ft) to south-facing windows (target azimuth 180° ±5°). The verifier checks for these properties and validates that no spurious overhangs are added to non-south windows. Currently, the model contains no overhang properties on any windows (baseline state). Simulation file generation is also required as part of task completion.
